items.title: Treasuries Slip Before Payrolls as Traders Weigh Warsh Comments
items.summary: Treasuries edged lower as traders awaited a US employment print that may give clues on the Federal Reserve’s interest-rate path after Chairman Kevin Warsh struck a dovish tone on inflation.

items.title: At least 13 killed in large-scale Russian missile and drone strikes on Kyiv
items.summary: Russia launched its biggest strikes on the city in weeks with children among the "significant number" of casualties, Ukrainian officials say.

items.title: Ukraine Takes the War to Russia
items.summary: Kyiv’s aim is simple: Put enough pressure on President Vladimir Putin that he agrees to end the war. Will it work?

items.title: China can help Ukraine recover from war and advance industry, ambassador says
items.summary:
China can provide “all-round” support for Ukraine’s post-war industrial recovery, Beijing’s envoy to Kyiv said while reaffirming his country’s commitment to pushing for a ceasefire and a political settlement for a conflict in its fifth year.
The remarks were made by China’s ambassador to Ukraine, Ma Shengkun, in an article published by the Interfax-Ukraine news agency on Tuesday, marking the 15th

items.title: Mass Mournings,6 Days and 2 Countries: Iran Prepares to Bury Supreme Leader
items.summary: Long-delayed funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, killed during U.S.-Israeli strikes at the war’s outset, are set to begin Friday. For the regime, it is a critical moment to demonstrate that it has endured.

China and US Target Tariff Reductions on Agricultural Products
items.summary: China and the US are seeking to roll back tariffs on some agricultural products, as they look to preserve a broader trade truce that was struck last year.

items.title: China’s Private Refiners Snap Up Middle East Oil as Prices Slide
items.summary: China’s independent refiners are taking advantage of cheaper Middle Eastern oil after flows accelerated through the Strait of Hormuz, snapping up barrels from producers including Saudi Arabia and Iraq.

items.title: Omani Fertilizer Firm Draws $12 Billion In Orders
items.summary: Oman India Fertiliser Co.’s initial public offering has drawn over $12 billion in orders, in the Middle East’s largest listing since the outbreak of the regional conflict. The fertilizer producer is set to raise $678 million via the share sale, Laura Gardner Cuesta reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: ЦБ: снижение ставки не означает, что Банк России и далее будет действовать такими шагами
Why Hong Kong has become this year’s worst-performing stock market
items.summary:
There are many disconnects in financial markets. One of them is Japan’s benchmark 10-year bond yield, which currently stands at just 2.7 per cent despite the country’s large public debt burden – more than 240 per cent of economic output.
